title: Malayalapuzha Devi Temple
tagline: Malayalapuzha Devi Temple — spiritual town of Kerala

overview.short: Malayalappuzha Devi Temple is a Bhadrakali temple situated at Malayalappuzha in Pathanamthitta in Kerala, India.
overview.description: Malayalappuzha Devi Temple is a Bhadrakali temple situated at Malayalappuzha in Pathanamthitta in Kerala, India. Nearest Railway station is Chengannur railway station It is believed that the temple was built more than 1000 years ago. In the temple, Bhadrakali is seen in a ferocious form soon after the killing of the demon, Darika. The main idol is 5.5 feet high, made from katu sarkara yogam.

routes.byAir: Fly to nearest airport + road transfer
Fly to nearest airport + road transfer

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Malayalappuzha
topPlaces.description: Malayalapuzha is a village outside the city suburbs of Pathanamthitta in Kerala. It is seven kilometers away from Pathanamthitta central junction and five kilometers from Kumbazha junction.

topPlaces.name: Mount Bethany EHSS
topPlaces.description: Mount Bethany English Medium Higher Secondary School is a school in Kerala, India. The school began providing education to lower primary level classes in the late 1970s.

topPlaces.name: Thekkummala
topPlaces.description: Thekkummala is a small hill village in Vadasserikara Panchayat in Pathanamthitta district, India.

topPlaces.name: Bethel Marthoma Church, Chengara
topPlaces.description: Bethel Marthoma Church is a Mar Thoma Syrian Church located at Chengara, Pathanamthitta, Kerala, India. Bethel Marthoma Church belongs to the Ranni Nilackal Diocese of Mar Thoma Syrian Church.

topPlaces.name: St. George's Church, Mylapra
topPlaces.description: Mylapra Valiyapalli or St. George Orthodox Church, Mylapra, referred to as Chakkittayil palli (ചക്കിട്ടേൽ പള്ളി) is a pilgrim center located in the village of Mylapra, in the India state of Kerala.

topPlaces.name: Sacred Heart Malankara Catholic Church, Mylapra
topPlaces.description: Sacred Heart Malankara Syrian Catholic Church or referred as S H Church is located in the village of Mylapra, near Pathanamthitta, on the road side of the Main Eastern Highway in the Indian state of Kerala. It is home to one of the first Syro-Malankara Catholic Church communities and is the biggest and oldest established church in the Eparchy of Pathanamthitta.

image.alt: Sacred Heart Malankara Catholic Church, Mylapra

topPlaces.name: Syro-Malankara Catholic Eparchy of Pathanamthitta
topPlaces.description: The Eparchy of Pathanamthitta is a Syro-Malankara Catholic Church ecclesiastical territory or eparchy of the Catholic Church in Kerala, India. The diocese was created by Pope Benedict XVI on 25 January 2010.

topPlaces.name: Sree Bhuvaneswary Temple, Valamchuzhy
topPlaces.description: Sree Bhuvaneswary Temple, Valamchuzy is one of the oldest temples in Pathanamthitta, South India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Malayalapuzha Devi Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Malayalapuzha Devi Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Malayalapuzha Devi Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Thiruvananthapuram International Airport (90 km) and the nearest railway station is Thiruvananthapuram Central (90 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Malayalapuzha Devi Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Malayalapuzha Devi Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Malayalapuzha Devi Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Malayalapuzha Devi Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Malayalapuzha Devi Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Malayalappuzha, Mount Bethany EHSS, Thekkummala, Bethel Marthoma Church, Chengara, St. George's Church, Mylapra.
